words.example: She complained about the noisy neighbor.
He seemed tired after the trip.
Why don't you come over for dinner?
He finally came to his senses.
Whose book is this on the table?
You should read at least one chapter daily.

words.portuguese: em vez de / prefiro / bastante / antes / preferivelmente
words.example: I would rather stay home tonight.
Despite the rain, we went hiking.

words.portuguese: justamente / razoalvelmente / bastante / de maneira justa
words.example: She performed fairly well in the exam.
Gotcha! I’ll meet you at noon.
It’s sort of cold today.

words.portuguese: bricando / zoando / bobear /desperdiçar tempo / não fazer nada produtivo
words.example: Stop goofing off and finish your work.

words.portuguese: dê uma olhada / ter um vislumbre
words.example: From the hill, we got a glimpse of the city.
The project is so far so good.
Look out! The bike is coming fast.

words.portuguese: Usado para indicar as horas inteiras no relógio (abreviação de "of the clock")
words.example: It’s three o’clock now.

words.portuguese: Mais distante, mais adiante ou mais
words.example: We need to discuss this further tomorrow.
That was a weird coincidence.
The library is a quiet place to study.
She is quite talented at music.
She hopes to achieve her goals this year.
Watch out for the gotchas in the contract.
I need another cup of coffee.
She is aware of the upcoming changes.
The vaccine was a major breakthrough.

words.portuguese: revisar / melhorar algo que já se sabe
words.example: She needs to brush up on French.
We walked along the quiet beach.

words.portuguese: cadela (literal) / usado também como um insulto (gíria)
words.example: She called him a nasty bitch.
The exam was really tough.

words.portuguese: embora / porém (no final da frase, dá ideia de mas)
words.example: It was cold, though we went out.
She had a sudden thought about travel.
We drove through the tunnel.
She gave a thorough explanation.
She traveled throughout Europe last year.
She brought cookies to the party.
He bought a new laptop yesterday.

words.portuguese: regra / norma ou princípio que deve ser seguido
words.example: The rule says no phones in class.

words.portuguese: função ou posição que alguém desempenha em um contexto específico
words.example: She played the lead role in the play.

words.portuguese: valor ou importância de algo ou alguém
words.example: The painting is worth a fortune.
He is claiming ownership of the patent.
Actually, I prefer tea over coffee.
In fact, the meeting was cancelled.
Take the bus instead of driving.
Since it rained, we stayed inside.
The flight was delayed due to fog.
It was raining; therefore, we stayed home.
Finish the report; in the meantime, call the client.
Never mind, I found the answer myself.
What a pity you missed the concert.
I'll be there pretty soon.
Hopefully the weather will improve.
You bet, I'll join you tomorrow.
I got it, the answer is 42.
She laughed as if she knew the secret.
Wake up early to catch the sunrise.
How come you’re late again?
My bad, I forgot to call you.
Besides coffee, I also like tea.
Throw away the broken glass.
The teacher is very strict about deadlines.
No way will I miss the flight.
The party got wild after midnight.
The kitten is very playful today.

words.portuguese: em relação a / a respeito de / sobre
words.example: Regarding your request, we will reply soon.
She relies on coffee every morning.
Hard work pays off in the end.
His greatest weakness is procrastination.
Yoga promotes overall wellness.
She will accomplish her goals this year.
As far as I know, the meeting is tomorrow.
As far as it goes, it's acceptable.
We need to narrow down the options.
The buzzing noise was really annoying.
The downside of the plan is cost.
He can be naive about market trends.
She is a reliable source of information.
The AI uses a reasoning engine for decisions.
The spy discovered a hidden enemy.
The bride walked down the aisle.
The groom waited nervously at the altar.
She faced many enemies during the war.
The brave firefighter entered the burning building.
She was afraid of the dark.
He always stirs the pot during meetings.
He wore a black beret on his head.
The new law was strictly enforced.
Every system has its drawbacks.
Electricity powers most modern devices.

sentences.portuguese: Onde os visitantes não podem ficar ?

sentences.english: That person said the newspaper was theirs
sentences.portuguese: Aquela pessoa disse que o jornal era deles

sentences.english: Sometimes being friendly with everyone pays off.
sentences.portuguese: Às vezes ser amigável com todos compensa.

sentences.english: Have you guys ever gone camping ?
He gave her a kiss on the cheek
sentences.portuguese: Ele deu um beijo na bochecha dela

sentences.english: Which bowl would you like your ice cream in ?
sentences.portuguese: Em qual tigela gostaria do seu sorvete ?

sentences.english: People are making fun of me for hugging my friends. They said it's too much.
sentences.portuguese: As pessoas estão tirando sarro de mim por abraçar meus amigos. Dizem que é demais.

sentences.english: Do you want to know something even cooler?
sentences.portuguese: Você quer saber de algo ainda mais legal?

sentences.english: Have all the students finished reviewing the lesson yet ?
sentences.portuguese: Todos os alunos já terminaram de rever a lição?

sentences.english: Have the kids fallen asleep yet ?
sentences.portuguese: As crianças já caíram no sono?

sentences.english: Have you ever fallen asleep in class ?
sentences.portuguese: Você caiu no sono na aula alguma vez?

sentences.english: Only six weeks' worth of research data
sentences.portuguese: Apenas seis semanas de dados de pesquisa

sentences.english: In Ireland, it's nice to bring flowers when you go to someone's house.
sentences.portuguese: Na Irlanda, é simpático levar flores quando você vai a casa de alguém.

sentences.english: Does anyone know what monkeys use stones for ?
sentences.portuguese: Alguém sabe para que os macacos usam pedras?
